sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, event_time DOUBLE, value NUMERIC);
SELECT to_timestamp(event_time * (SELECT AVG(value) FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL)) AS computed_timestamp FROM test_data WHERE event_time IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, AVG(value * 2), to_timestamp(event_time) FROM test_data GROUP BY id HAVING AVG(value * 2) > 50;
SELECT t.id, to_timestamp(t.event_time) * 2 AS double_timestamp FROM test_data t WHERE t.value IS NOT NULL AND (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data WHERE value IS NULL) > 0;
WITH computed AS (SELECT id, to_timestamp(event_time * 10) AS multiplied_timestamp FROM test_data WHERE event_time IS NOT NULL) SELECT AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM multiplied_timestamp)) FROM computed;
SELECT *, to_timestamp((event_time + CASE WHEN value IS NOT NULL THEN value ELSE 0 END) * 5) FROM test_data WHERE event_time IS NOT NULL AND value IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE temp_data (timestamp_value TIMESTAMP, key INTEGER);
SELECT key, AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM timestamp_value)) FROM temp_data GROUP BY key HAVING key IS NOT NULL;
SELECT t.id, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_data) * to_timestamp(event_time) AS complex_timestamp FROM test_data t WHERE event_time BETWEEN 1284352320 AND 1284352325;
